cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this question. The user provided a passage about the class system in British society and a multiple-choice question. The question is asking: According to the passage, you cannot judge an Englishman's social class by which of the factors listed? The options are (A) where he was born, (B) the way he speaks, (C) the clothes he wears, (D) the interests he has. First, I need to refer back to the passage. The first paragraph says, "Most British people grow up with a deep knowledge and understanding of the class system even if they are not very conscious of it. Most people know which class they belong to by the way they speak, their clothes, their interests or even the type of food they eat."
